fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 68 uses strict greater-than (`now > effective`) instead of greater-than-or-equal (`now >= effective`), so when `now == effective` the entry is incorrectly treated as still valid rather than expired.

constraints.intent: When now equals effective, the function must return None (expired); the strict > comparison fails this.
After computing effective, verify that the subsequent expiry check will use >= semantics (now >= effective means expired).
In the else branch (entry considered valid), now must be strictly less than effective; equality should have triggered expiry.
Confirm effective is a numeric value before the comparison so the boundary check is meaningful.
